response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with roughly 17 years of experience, but the role is fundamentally a deep C++ engineering position in industrial automation and the candidate's C++ exposure is minimal — limited to a 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. The job's core requirements around IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT), multi-threaded systems design, and OT/IoT domain knowledge are entirely absent from the resume. While Node.js and React are listed as advantageous skills and the candidate has them, those alone cannot compensate for the missing primary technical foundation.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Node.js, TypeScript, and React skills directly match the advantageous (nice-to-have) stack listed in the JD
Consistent Agile and software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is a single 6-month position from 2014–2015 with no evidence of modern C++14/17/20, STL depth, or concurrent programming at the level required
Zero industrial automation, OT/IoT, or relevant protocol experience (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT) — the entire domain context of the role is absent
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle practices, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or any embedded/industrial security context
